---
title: "Business Plan"
subtitle: "Serious Ben Entertainment"
author:
  name: "Benjamin Gross & Phelire Chisi"
  email: ben@seriousbenentertainment.org
  affiliation:
    - Serious Ben Entertainment
    - "Email: ben@seriousbenentertainment.org"
    - "Website: https://www.seriousbenentertainment.org"
filters:
  - topics/adjustTime.lua
abstract: |
  This business plan covers the establishment of Serious Ben Entertainment, a company focused on providing innovative entertainment solutions. The plan outlines the company's mission, market analysis, product offerings, marketing strategy, financial projections, and operational plan. The goal is to create a sustainable business that delivers high-quality entertainment experiences to our target audience while achieving profitability and growth.
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true
    theme: cerulean
    toc: true
    toc-expand: 5
    toc-depth: 5
    number-sections: false
    number-depth: 5
  pdf:
    keep-tex: false
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        \usepackage[noblocks]{authblk}
        \renewcommand*{\Authsep}{, }
        \renewcommand*{\Authand}{, }
        \renewcommand*{\Authands}{, }
        \renewcommand\Affilfont{\small}
    documentclass: scrartcl
    classoption: [onecolumn, oneside, a4paper]
    linkcolor: blue
    urlcolor: blue
    filecolor: magenta
    citecolor: magenta
    colorlinks: true
    margin-left: "1in"
    margin-right: "1in"
    margin-top: "1in"
    margin-bottom: "1in"
    papersize: a4
    fig-cap-location: top
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 5
    number-sections: false
    lof: false
    lot: false
link-citations: true
csl: "./topics/apa.csl"
bibliography: "./topics/bibliography.bib"
suppress-bibliography: false
execute:
  enabled: true
  echo: false
---

\clearpage

::: callout-note
## Founding Proposal

**Founding:** Serious Ben Entertainment is a new company focused on providing innovative health entertainment solutions. The company will be founded by Benjamin Gross and Phelire Chisi, who bring a unique combination of skills and experience in the entertainment and education industry. The company will be based in Lilongwe, Malawi, and will serve a global english speaking audience through digital platforms.

**Founders:** Benjamin Gross & Phelire Chisi
New Area 43
Lilongwe
:::

# Brief Project Description {#sec-kurzbeschreibung}

Serious Ben Entertainment is developing a web-based SaaS platform for digital health coaching: certified §20 prevention courses (e.g., back-school or relaxation techniques), (AI-supported) coaching, and free diagnostic tools for lead generation. The target audiences are private individuals, statutory health insurance funds, and companies (workplace health management).

## Business Idea {#sec-geschaeftsidee}

I am founding **Serious Ben Entertainment**, a digital company that develops and offers innovative SaaS solutions (Software as a Service) in the field of (health) coaching and prevention. The focus is on developing and providing web-based applications via the platform [**www.seriousbenentertainment.org**](https://www.seriousbenentertainment.org), which support people with low-threshold access to health promotion and personal development — from preventive health courses through to AI-supported and classical coaching.

The business model is based on four pillars, with Pillar 4 serving to secure financial stability in the start-up phase:

1.  **Certified §20 SGB V prevention courses as digital apps** (back-school, relaxation techniques, Qi-Gong) priced at 150 Euros, of which statutory health insurance funds reimburse 75–150 Euros.
2.  **AI-supported and classical coaching** for holistic health promotion and personal development.
3.  **Free diagnostic mini-apps** (questionnaires, personality games) as entry-level products that, through evidence-based evaluations, reveal development potential and advertise and broker paid coaching services.
4.  **Lecturing at Hochschule Fresenius** to secure a stable income during the founding phase (Year 1 / 2).

The technical implementation of the services is delivered via:

-   An **Angular-based web application** ([www.seriousbenentertainment.org](https://www.seriousbenentertainment.org)) as the central SaaS platform
    -   **Serious Games** for playful health promotion.
    -   **Conversational AI and AI-supported analyses** for personalized recommendations.
-   **Scalable cloud infrastructure** (e.g., Snowflake Cloud) for flexible service delivery without (high) fixed costs.
-   **In-house development** supported by my own developer skills, GitHub Copilot, and other development tools to avoid high costs, as well as best practices that I learned during my time at adesso SE.

![Website – Health Courses](./topics/Webpage.png)

![Website – Shop](./topics/Webpage_Shop.png)

![Website – Course](./topics/Webpage_Kurs.png)

::: callout-important
## Flagship Product

The **flagship product** is the **BenBox RS (back-school)**, a certified §20 prevention program designed as a Serious Game for the prevention of back pain and musculoskeletal limitations. As a web-based SaaS application, it combines playful elements with evidence-based exercises (e.g., Brügger Therapy) and posture detection (a self-developed deep learning model — CNN), and is offered at a price of 150 Euros, with health insurance funds reimbursing up to 150 Euros. This and other prevention courses, such as relaxation techniques or Qi-Gong, are provided as digital apps via the platform. The Central Examination Office for Prevention (ZPP) certifies these services in accordance with §20 SGB V, e.g., in the category [Game-based learning/serious games/e-teaching](https://www.zentrale-pruefstelle-praevention.de/leistungen/praeventionsangebot-zertifizieren-lassen/ikt-angebote/zertifizierbare-ikt-formate/).
:::

### Target Groups and Positioning

The SaaS platform is primarily aimed at:

-   **Statutory health insurance funds** as partners (these will be a central market access channel, see *1.4.4 Marketing and Sales – Cooperations and Partnerships*) for §20 SGB V prevention courses
-   **Health-conscious private individuals** as direct end customers of the SaaS platform
-   **Companies in the workplace health management (WHM) context** for employee health
-   **Coaching clients** for personal and AI-supported development support

As founder, I position myself not only as a (digital) therapist, trainer, and coach, but also as a **developer and provider of digital health products** in cooperation with therapy practices. Through my many years of experience in the development and implementation of e-Health products (2019–2025), as well as my studies in Data Science (2023–2026), I can offer professionally tailored solutions of low to medium complexity for the management and analysis of health data. I draw my professional health expertise from my physiotherapy training (2004–2007), my degree in Health Economics (2017–2019), and the numerous therapy and coaching trainings (e.g., Alexander Technique or Burnout Counseling). All of these competencies feed into the development of intelligent, scalable SaaS solutions, with the support of current AI assistants.

The business model uses modern cloud technologies (e.g., Snowflake) for flexible scalability (compute and data storage) and enables location-independent use of all services via the web platform [**www.seriousbenentertainment.org**](https://www.seriousbenentertainment.org), with fixed base costs and minimal hardware investment.

## Markets {#sec-maerkte}

The market for digital health applications (DiGA) and preventive health promotion offerings (e.g., prevention under §20 SGB V) is growing continuously. The COVID-19 pandemic has further accelerated the acceptance of digital health solutions. Added to this is the AI revolution, which is increasing curiosity about AI-driven health solutions. At the same time, the need for low-threshold prevention offerings is rising due to:

-   Demographic change and increasing life expectancy
-   Rise of stress-related illnesses and psychological strain
-   Lack of exercise and back complaints in work and everyday life
-   The general digitalization of healthcare

The founding of **Serious Ben Entertainment** is also based on my many years of experience as a service provider in the healthcare system (physiotherapist, personal trainer, course leader, coach, and managing director of a therapy center), where I repeatedly recognized the limits of traditional care structures. The development of digital, scalable solutions makes it possible to overcome these limitations and to offer innovative health promotion. I have developed digital products in different tech stacks, e.g., as part of my bachelor's thesis (Health Economics) — an iPad serious game that I exhibited at Gamescom in 2019. Another was a data portal for Kamuzu Central Hospital in Lilongwe, Malawi, with AI-supported assistance.

### Market Potentials

The market for digital health services offers significant growth potential:

-   **§20 SGB V Prevention:** Health insurance funds invest several hundred million euros annually in prevention courses. The shift to digital is being strongly promoted.
-   **Workplace Health Promotion (WHM):** Companies are increasingly looking for scalable, digital solutions for employee health
-   **Coaching market:** The market for (health) coaching is growing continuously; AI-supported approaches open up new possibilities
-   **SaaS model for providers:** Recurring revenue through subscriptions and product sales of e-Health solutions developed for customers

### Professional Foundations

My interdisciplinary education forms the ideal basis for developing evidence-based digital health products:

-   **Physiotherapy, training, course provision:** Sound knowledge of movement, posture, and body awareness
-   **Alexander Technique, burnout counseling & coaching:** Expertise in mental health and behavior change
-   **Health Economics (B.Sc.):** Understanding of healthcare systems, billing models, and market mechanisms
-   **Data Science (M.Sc.):** Technical competence for software development, AI development, data analysis, and cloud infrastructure, as well as the development of a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) for posture and movement detection
-   **Lecturer in Physiotherapy & Data Science:** Didactic skills for target-group-appropriate knowledge transfer

This mix enables me to independently develop (as a solopreneur) SaaS products that combine therapeutic quality with technical innovation and economic viability.

### Market Overview

```{mermaid}
%%| fig-cap: "Serious Ben Entertainment Product Portfolio"
%%| fig-width: 6
%%| fig-height: 4
graph TD
    A[Serious Ben Entertainment<br/>SaaS Platform] --> B[§20 Prevention Apps / Courses]
    A --> C[Coaching Services]
    A --> D[Free Diagnostic Apps]

    B --> B1[BenBox RS<br/>Back-School]
    B --> B2[Relaxation<br/>Techniques]
    B --> B3[Qi-Gong]

    C --> C1[Coaching]
    C --> C2[AI-supported<br/>Coaching]

    D --> D1[Personality<br/>Questionnaires]
    D --> D2[Mini-Games for<br/>Self-Reflection]
```

## Personal and Professional Qualifications {#sec-qualifikationen}

### Education and Training

**Excerpt from health and therapy training:**

-   **State-certified physiotherapist** (2004–2007)
-   **Alexander Technique teacher** (2009–2013, four-year training)
-   **Burnout counselor** and coach
-   Diplom Brügger Therapist
-   PhysioCORE® Personal Trainer
-   Relaxation trainer (§20 SGB V certified)
-   Back-school instructor (§20 SGB V certified)
-   Qi-Gong teacher (§20 SGB V certified)

**Excerpt of academic qualifications:**

-   **Master of Science Data Science** (2023–2026, University of Edinburgh, UK)
-   **Bachelor of Science Health Economics** (2017–2019, Hochschule Fresenius)
-   **Train the Trainer** – Adult Education (2024)
-   **Future Work Agent** – Change Management & New Work (2020–2021)

**Excerpt of IT-technical certifications:**

-   **SnowPro Associate: Platform** (Snowflake, 2025) – Cloud Data Platform
-   **adesso certified GenAI Fundamentals** (2025) – Generative AI and LLMs
-   **ISTQB® Certified Tester Foundation Level** (2025) – Software Testing
-   **Professional Product Owner** (Scrum.org, 2024) – Agile Product Management

Further details can be found in the attached CV.

### Professional Experience and Competencies

**Current position:**

-   **Lecturer at Hochschule Fresenius** (since 09/2025): Courses in Analytical Skills for Business and Data Science and Data Analytics

**Relevant professional experience:**

-   **Senior IT Consultant at adesso SE** (03/2024–09/2025): Data Engineering, AI development, Snowflake data platforms, RAG and agent systems
-   **Technical Advisor, Ministry of Health Malawi** (01/2022–12/2023): Data Engineering & AI in healthcare, development of health data platforms
-   **Technical Contract Manager, Deutscher Hausärzteverband** (11/2019–06/2021): Data Engineering, project management of digital health solutions
-   **Lecturer Ludwig Fresenius Schule** (07/2016–10/2019): Teaching and eLearning development
-   **Managing Director therapie centrum Rosenheim** (09/2013–06/2016): Corporate management, strategy, staff leadership
-   **Physiotherapist & Lecturer** (11/2007–08/2013): Therapy, workplace health management, personal training

**Core technical competencies:**

-   **Programming:** Python, R, JavaScript/TypeScript, Angular
-   **Data Engineering:** Snowflake, SQL, ETL processes, data modeling
-   **AI/ML:** TensorFlow, Hugging Face, OpenAI API, RAG systems
-   **DevOps:** Docker, Git/GitHub, CI/CD
-   **Health IT:** HL7v2, FHIR, HIS/PMS systems

**Pedagogical and coaching competencies:**

-   Extensive teaching experience at universities and vocational schools
-   Adult education and didactic preparation of complex content
-   Coaching training (Alexander Technique, burnout counseling)
-   Experience in developing eLearning content and Serious Games

## Customer Target Groups, Competition, Marketing, and Pricing {#sec-kunden-marketing}

### Customer Target Groups

The SaaS offerings of Serious Ben Entertainment are aimed at different target groups with specific needs:

**Primary target groups:**

-   **Health-conscious private individuals** (25–65 years) interested in prevention and self-care
-   **People with back complaints** or stress strain who, due to time constraints, prefer digital solutions
-   **Coaching-interested individuals** who pursue personal or professional development

**Institutional partners:**

-   **Statutory health insurance funds** for §20 SGB V prevention courses in partnership (this will be a central market access channel, see *1.4.4 Marketing and Sales – Cooperations and Partnerships*)
-   **Companies** within the framework of Workplace Health Promotion (WHM)
-   **HR departments** for employee wellness programs

### Competitive Positioning

The market for digital health applications is growing strongly but is also becoming increasingly fragmented. Different providers focus on fitness apps, meditation apps, or pure coaching platforms.

**Unique selling points of Serious Ben Entertainment:**

1.  **Interdisciplinary expertise:** Combination of therapeutic know-how, Data Science (in healthcare), and coaching competence
2.  **Holistic approach:** Integration of movement, relaxation, coaching, and AI support in one platform
3.  **Evidence-based:** All offerings are based on scientifically sound methods
4.  **Freemium strategy:** Free diagnostic apps as a low-threshold entry point
5.  **Health-insurance certification:** §20 SGB V recognition enables reimbursement for customers and allows higher base prices
6.  **Personal touch:** Combination of automated AI services and personal coaching (Blended Learning)

**Competitive comparison:**

-   Pure fitness apps (e.g., Freeletics): Focus only on training, no coaching
-   Meditation apps (e.g., Headspace): Mental health only, no movement
-   Coaching platforms: Mostly high cost, no health-insurance integration
-   §20 course providers: Mostly classic on-site courses, little digital innovation

### Pricing

The pricing structure is transparent and follows the SaaS model with various price tiers:

**§20 Prevention Apps:**

-   Price: **EUR 150.00** per course (8–10 weeks)
-   Reimbursement by statutory health insurance funds: **EUR 75–150**
-   Customer's own contribution: **EUR 0–75**
-   Offerings: BenBox RS (back-school), relaxation techniques, Qi-Gong

**Coaching Services:**

-   **AI-supported coaching:** Monthly subscription EUR 30.00
-   **Coaching:** EUR 125.00 per session (60 min.)
-   **Coaching package:** EUR 650.00 (6 sessions)

**Free offerings (lead generation):**

-   Personality questionnaires
-   Mini self-reflection games
-   Health check apps

The pricing takes into account:

-   Standard market rates for digital health offerings
-   Reimbursability by health insurance funds
-   Low entry barriers through free diagnostic tools
-   Value perception through evidence-based quality

### Marketing and Sales

**Online Marketing:**

-   **Website:** [www.seriousbenentertainment.org](https://www.seriousbenentertainment.org) as the central SaaS platform
-   **Content marketing:** Blog articles on health topics, SEO optimization
-   **Social media:** LinkedIn (B2B), Instagram (B2C)
-   **E-mail marketing:** Newsletter with health tips

**Cooperations and Partnerships:**

-   **Health-insurance partnerships for §20 courses:** Acquiring health insurance funds as partners is central to market access, as they offer an enormous multiplier effect. Outreach proceeds in stages:
    1.  *Certification by the ZPP:* First, the BenBox RS (back-school) is certified by the Central Examination Office for Prevention (ZPP) as an ICT-based prevention offering. This is a prerequisite for reimbursability.
    2.  *Direct outreach to prevention departments:* After successful certification, the prevention departments of selected health insurance funds (e.g., AOK, TK, Barmer) are contacted directly — by e-mail and telephone with a concrete offer and certification proof.
    3.  *Cooperation models:*
        -   **Listing in the course directory:** Health insurance funds list certified courses in their online directories; insured persons can book these directly and apply for reimbursement.
        -   **Framework agreements:** Long-term agreements with individual funds enabling preferred placement, joint marketing campaigns, or exclusive offers for their insured.
        -   **White-label solutions:** Individual customization of the apps (branding, content) for health insurance funds that wish to offer their own digital prevention services under their name.
    4.  *Network maintenance:* Participation in industry events (e.g., DMEA, WHM congresses) for contact-building and product presentation.
-   **WHM providers and corporate cooperations:** Companies in the field of Workplace Health Management (WHM) are addressed as multipliers. Cooperation forms include the integration of SaaS offerings into existing WHM programs, joint offering design for corporate customers, and training for HR departments.
-   **Health portals and directories:** Listing in relevant online directories (e.g., Präventionskurse.de, health-insurance course searches) as well as cooperations with health portals for content distribution and lead generation.

**Freemium Strategy:**

-   Free apps as an entry point and trust-builder — users experience the added value without a financial hurdle and develop trust in the platform.
-   Data-driven, personalized recommendations for premium offerings — based on the results of the free diagnostic tools, tailored paid courses or coaching services are suggested.
-   Evidence-based evaluations show concrete added value — scientifically grounded result reports motivate users to book further offerings.

**Sales channels:**

-   Direct sales via own website – main channel for all products with integrated booking and payment system.
-   Health-insurance directories for §20 courses – central visibility for insured persons actively looking for reimbursable prevention offerings.
-   App stores (planned for mobile apps) – expansion of reach through presence in iOS App Store and Google Play from 2027.
-   B2B sales to companies – direct outreach to HR and WHM managers for company licenses and employee offerings.

### Offering Overview

```{mermaid}
%%| fig-cap: "Serious Ben Entertainment Range of Offerings"
%%| fig-width: 6
%%| fig-height: 3.5
graph LR
    A[Serious Ben Entertainment<br/>SaaS Platform] --> B[§20 Prevention Apps<br/>€150]
    A --> C[Coaching]
    A --> D[Free Tools]

    B --> B1[Back-School]
    B --> B2[Relaxation<br/>Techniques]
    B --> B3[Qi-Gong]

    C --> C1[Coaching<br/>€125/session]
    C --> C2[AI Coaching<br/>€30.00/month]

    D --> D1[Questionnaires]
    D --> D2[Mini-Games]
```

## Future Outlook, Opportunities, and Risks {#sec-zukunftsaussichten}

### Market Entry Strategy

The productive launch of the SaaS platform [**www.seriousbenentertainment.org**](https://www.seriousbenentertainment.org) is planned for **early 2026** — it is already publicly accessible in version 1.0.0 (Beta). The strategy follows a multi-stage approach:

**Phase 1 (Q3 2026): Soft Launch**

-   Publication of the website with free diagnostic apps
-   Launch of the first §20 app (BenBox RS Back-School)
-   Building the first user base through the freemium strategy

**Phase 2 (Q4 2026 – Q1 2027): Product Expansion**

-   Launch of further §20 apps (relaxation techniques, Qi-Gong)
-   Integration of the AI-supported coaching service
-   Start of cooperations with health insurance funds

**Phase 3 (Q2 2027 onwards): Scaling**

-   Expansion of B2B sales channels (WHM)
-   Development of mobile apps for iOS/Android
-   International expansion (English-language versions)

### Unique Selling Points and Success Factors

**Technological strengths:**

-   Cloud-based, scalable infrastructure (Snowflake)
-   AI-supported personalization and coaching
-   Modern web technologies (Angular) for optimal user experience

**Professional strengths:**

-   Unique combination of health expertise and Data Science
-   §20 SGB V certification enables health-insurance reimbursement
-   Evidence-based methods with scientific grounding

**Business model:**

-   Recurring revenue through SaaS subscriptions
-   Low customer acquisition costs through the freemium strategy
-   Scalability without proportional cost increase

### Opportunities

1.  **Growing market:** Digital health is booming, reinforced by demographic change
2.  **Health-insurance integration:** §20 certification creates trust and lowers price barriers
3.  **AI trends:** Growing interest in AI-supported solutions
4.  **WHM potential:** Companies are increasingly investing in employee health
5.  **Network:** Existing contacts from previous activities in the healthcare sector
6.  **Lecturing:** Lecturing at Hochschule Fresenius strengthens reputation and offers synergy effects

### Risks and Mitigation

**Business risks:**

-   **Customer acquisition:** Slow build-up of the user base → mitigated by the freemium model and targeted marketing
-   **Competition:** New providers → continuous innovation and quality assurance
-   **Technical complexity:** Development effort → agile development, MVP approach
-   **Regulation:** Changes to §20 SGB V → diversification of revenue sources

**Financial mitigation:**

-   **Side activity as lecturer:** Stable base income parallel to founding
-   **Low capital requirement:** Digital business model without high investment costs
-   **Flexibility:** If needed, return to employment (physiotherapy, IT consulting) is possible
-   **Personal situation:** Single, no maintenance obligations, modest lifestyle

**Operational risks:**

-   **Data protection:** Health data → strict GDPR compliance, secure cloud infrastructure
-   **Quality assurance:** Therapeutic quality → regular evaluations, user feedback
-   **Scaling:** Growth management → cloud infrastructure enables automatic scaling

### Personal Prerequisites

My personal situation favors the founding venture:

-   **Financial flexibility:** Through a modest lifestyle and no financial obligations, well-equipped for the start-up phase (2026 to 2027)
-   **Professional experience:** Prior founding and leadership experience (therapie centrum Rosenheim)
-   **Resilience:** Experience with financially challenging phases during further training, vocational training, and studies
-   **Network:** Long-standing nationwide contacts in healthcare and the health IT industry
-   **Continuous further training:** Commitment to lifelong learning and adaptability

# Profitability Calculations {#sec-wirtschaftlichkeit}

## Calculation of Required Personal Drawings {#sec-privatentnahmen}

### Cost of Living (monthly)

| Item                                                  | Amount (€)   |
|-------------------------------------------------------|--------------|
| Apartment (incl. heating and all incidental costs)    | 670.00       |
| Food, beverages, and stimulants                       | 250.00       |
| Clothing and shoes                                    | 50.00        |
| Furnishings                                           | 20.00        |
| Health and personal care                              | 15.00        |
| Transport (public transport + road bike accessories)  | 65.00        |
| Communication, leisure, and culture                   | 40.00        |
| Education                                             | 50.00        |
| Other                                                 | 20.00        |
| **Subtotal cost of living monthly**                   | **1,180.00** |

### Insurance and Provident Contributions

| Item                                                  | Amount (€) |
|-------------------------------------------------------|------------|
| Statutory or private health and long-term care insurance | 363.00  |
| Statutory or private pension/retirement provision     | 00.00      |
| Other insurance                                       | 11.00      |
| **Total monthly**                                     | **374.00** |

## Operational Calculations {#sec-betriebliche-berechnungen}

### Capital Requirement Plan in the Start-up Phase (Assumption: 3 months)

#### Pre-founding

| Item                                                       | Amount (€) |
|------------------------------------------------------------|------------|
| Start-up advisory                                          | 200.00     |
| Registrations / notifications / permits (Trade Office, IHK) | 100.00    |
| Legal advice (data protection, T&Cs)                       | 300.00     |
| Patent, license, and franchise fees                        | 0.00       |
| **Total one-time founding costs**                          | **600.00** |

#### Ongoing Business Costs (per month)

| Item                                                | Amount (€)   |
|-----------------------------------------------------|--------------|
| Further training / certifications                   | 50.00        |
| Cloud hosting (STRATO V-Server, Snowflake)          | 50.00        |
| Domain & website hosting                            | 15.00        |
| Marketing and advertising (online ads, SEO)         | 300.00       |
| Office supplies incl. postage                       | 20.00        |
| Business insurance (professional liability)         | 40.00        |
| Contributions and fees                              | 50.00        |
| Telecommunications (internet, telephone)            | 30.00        |
| Tax advisor / bookkeeping                           | 150.00       |
| Other                                               | 50.00        |
| **Total ongoing operating expenses (monthly)**      | **755**      |
| **Total operating expenses (3 months)**             | **2,265.00** |

**Total capital requirement (start-up phase 3 months):** EUR 7,500

## Capital Requirement and Financing {#sec-kapitalbedarf}

### Financing Plan

| Item                                       | Amount (€)     |
|--------------------------------------------|----------------|
| **Equity**                                 |                |
| Bank balance                               | 17,500.00      |
| Private loans                              | 0.00           |
| Contributions in kind (existing IT equipment) | 5,000.00    |
| **Total own funds**                        | **22,500.00**  |
| **Outside capital**                        |                |
| Founding subsidy (6 months × €2,000)       | 12,000.00      |
| Long-term loans                            | 0.00           |
| Short-term loans                           | 0.00           |
| **Total outside capital / funding**        | **12,000.00**  |
| **Total financing**                        | **34,500.00**  |
| less total capital requirement (3 months)  | -7,500.00      |
| **Surplus / shortfall**                    | **+27,000.00** |

**Notes:**

-   Equity comprises existing cash and IT equipment (laptop, software)
-   Founding subsidy: 6 months × €2,000 = €12,000 (main funding)
-   The surplus serves as a liquidity reserve for further development

## Revenue, Cost, and Profit Forecast {#sec-umsatz-kosten-gewinn}

### Revenue Planning

The revenue forecast is based on realistic assumptions for a digital SaaS business model in the build-up phase:

**Assumptions Year 1 (2026, 6 months):**

-   §20 apps: 15 courses × €150 = €2,250
-   Coaching: 7.2 sessions × €125 = €900
-   AI Coaching subscriptions: 24 subs × €30 × ø 1 month = €718
-   **Total:** €3,868

**Assumptions Year 2 (2027):**

-   §20 apps: 60 courses × €150 = €9,000
-   Coaching: 36 sessions × €120 = €4,320
-   AI Coaching subscriptions: 30 subs × €30 × 12 months = €10,764
-   WHM cooperation: 2 companies × €2,000 = €4,000
-   **Total:** €28,084

**Assumptions Year 3 (2028):**

-   §20 apps: 120 courses × €150 = €18,000
-   Coaching: 60 sessions × €120 = €7,200
-   AI Coaching subscriptions: 80 subs × €30 × 12 months = €28,704
-   WHM cooperations: 5 companies × €2,500 = €12,500
-   **Total:** €66,404

### Chart: Company Development (Euro)

| Year | Revenue (€)      | Operating Costs (€) | Annual Surplus (€)   |
|------|------------------|---------------------|----------------------|
| 2026 | 3,868            | 4,530               | -662                 |
| 2027 | 28,084           | 9,060               | 19,024               |
| 2028 | 66,404           | 9,060               | 57,344               |

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| eval: true
#| label: fig-unternehmensentwicklung
#| fig-cap: "Revenue and Profit Development 2026-2028"
library(tidyverse)

# Creating data
jahre <- c("2026\n(6 mo.)", "2027", "2028")
umsatz <- c(3868, 28084, 66404)
kosten <- c(4530, 9060, 9060)
gewinn <- c(-662, 19024, 57344)

df <- tibble(
  Year = factor(jahre, levels = jahre),
  Revenue = umsatz,
  `Operating Costs` = kosten,
  `Annual Surplus` = gewinn
)

# Creating plot
df_long <- df |>
  pivot_longer(cols = -Year, names_to = "Category", values_to = "Amount")

ggplot(df_long, aes(x = Year, y = Amount, fill = Category)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Revenue" = "#2E7D32",
                                "Operating Costs" = "#C62828",
                                "Annual Surplus" = "#1976D2")) +
  labs(title = "Revenue and Profit Development 2026-2028",
       x = "Year",
       y = "Amount (€)",
       fill = "") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")
```

**Notes:**

-   Year 1 (6 months): Build-up phase with low revenues and a slightly negative operating result (-€662), but positive overall profitability through the founding subsidy and side income
-   Year 2: Strong revenue growth and clearly positive annual surplus
-   Year 3: Further significant revenue growth through an established product range and customer loyalty

## Liquidity Forecast {#sec-liquiditaetsvorschau}

### Income and Expenses (extrapolated and estimated)

| Item | 2026 (6 mo.) (€) | Year 2027 (€)   | Year 2028 (€)   |
|------------------|-------------------|------------------|------------------|
| **Net income** |  |  |  |
| §20 prevention apps | 2,250.00 | 9,000.00 | 18,000.00 |
| Coaching | 900.00 | 4,320.00 | 7,200.00 |
| AI Coaching subscriptions | 718.00 | 10,764.00 | 28,704.00 |
| WHM cooperations | 0.00 | 4,000.00 | 12,500.00 |
| **Total income = Gross income I** | **3,868.00** | **28,084.00** | **66,404.00** |
| less operating expenses | 4,530.00 | 9,060.00 | 9,060.00 |
| **Total operating result = Gross income II** | **-662.00** | **19,024.00** | **57,344.00** |
| Depreciation on investments | 400.00 | 400.00 | 400.00 |
| **Total basis for income tax** | **-1,062.00** | **18,624.00** | **56,944.00** |
| Personal drawings / cost of living | 9,324.00 | 18,648.00 | 18,648.00 |
| **Total operating result less personal drawings = Cash flow** | **-9,986.00** | **376.00** | **38,696.00** |
| \+ Side income (lecturing) | 4,000.00 | 8,000.00 | 8,000.00 |
| \+ Founding subsidy (6 months × €2,000) | 12,000.00 | 0.00 | 0.00 |
| **Total profitability** | **6,014.00** | **8,376.00** | **46,696.00** |
| **Cumulative cash flow** | **6,014.00** | **14,390.00** | **61,086.00** |

**Notes:**

**2026 (founding year, 6 months):**

-   Start-up phase with low revenues (€3,868)
-   Operating costs: 6 months × €755 = €4,530
-   Operating cash flow negative (-€662), but through founding subsidy (€12,000) and side income (€4,000) positive overall profitability of €6,014
-   Personal drawings: 6 months × (€1,180 cost of living + €374 insurance) = €9,324
-   Founding subsidy: 6 × €2,000 = €12,000

**2027 (Year 2):**

-   Significant revenue growth to €28,084
-   Operating costs: 12 months × €755 = €9,060
-   Operating annual surplus: €19,024
-   Personal drawings: 12 months × €1,554 = €18,648
-   Despite a positive operating result, low cash flow (€376) after personal drawings
-   With side income (€8,000), positive overall profitability: €8,376

**2028 (Year 3):**

-   Strong revenue growth to €66,404
-   Operating costs remain constant: €9,060 (assumption: no scaling of fixed costs)
-   Operating annual surplus: €57,344
-   Positive cash flow of €38,696 after personal drawings
-   Overall profitability incl. side income: €46,696
-   Cumulative cash flow: €61,086

**Liquidity Safeguarding:**

-   Founding subsidy secures the first 6 months
-   Side income from university lecturing in all three years
-   Equity reserve of €22,500 available for bridging
-   Low capital requirement due to digital business model
-   Positive overall profitability in all periods
-   If needed: return to employment (physiotherapy/IT) possible at any time

# Marketing Materials & Market Access {#sec-werbemittel}

## SaaS Platform {#sec-saas-plattform}

**Homepage:** [www.seriousbenentertainment.org](https://www.seriousbenentertainment.org)

The central web platform serves as the hub for all services:

**Technical implementation:**

-   **Frontend:** Angular-based single-page application
-   **Backend:** Cloud-native architecture (Snowflake, Node.js)
-   **AI integration:** Conversational AI, personalized recommendations
-   **Responsive design:** Optimized for desktop, tablet, and mobile

**Functions:**

-   User registration and management
-   Access to §20 prevention apps
-   AI-supported coaching dashboard
-   Free diagnostic tools and questionnaires
-   Booking system for coaching
-   Health-insurance billing and certificate management
-   Blog with health tips (content marketing)

## Product Portfolio {#sec-produktportfolio}

### §20 Prevention Apps (€150 each)

**1. BenBox RS (Back-School) – Flagship Product**

-   Serious Game for back health
-   8–10 weeks structured program
-   Evidence-based exercises
-   Gamification elements
-   Certified under §20 SGB V

**2. Relaxation Techniques**

-   Progressive muscle relaxation
-   Mindfulness exercises
-   Audio-supported sessions

**3. Qi-Gong**

-   Video-based movement instructions
-   Step-by-step tutorials
-   Traditional Chinese movement art

### Coaching Services

**AI-supported Coaching (€30.00/month):**

-   Conversational AI for daily reflection
-   Personalized development plans
-   Behavior tracking and feedback
-   Evidence-based recommendations

**Coaching (€125/session):**

-   60-minute individual sessions
-   Video coaching via secure platform
-   Holistic approach (body, mind, behavior)
-   Focus: burnout prevention, work-life balance, leadership skills

### Free Lead Generation

**Diagnostic Apps:** - Personality questionnaires (e.g., burnout risk) - Back check - Stress level assessment - Mini self-reflection games

After evaluation: Personalized recommendations for paid offerings

## Mobile App (planned) {#sec-mobile-app}

**App Name:** BenBox

**Status:** Development planned for 2027

**Functions:** - All web functions as a native app - Offline use of selected content - Push notifications for coaching - Integration with Apple Health / Google Fit

# Appendix {#sec-anhang}

## Information on the Founding Subsidy {#sec-gruenderzuschuss}

The founding subsidy from the Federal Employment Agency is a central funding measure for new business creation. The application is made in two phases:

**Phase 1 (mandatory benefit if requirements are met):**

-   Duration: 6 months
-   Amount: Most recently received unemployment benefit + €300 lump sum
-   In my case: approx. €1,700 ALG I + €300 = **€2,000 monthly**
-   Total: **€12,000** for 6 months

**Phase 2 (discretionary benefit):**

-   Duration: a further 9 months
-   Amount: €300 monthly
-   Total: €2,700 (if granted)

**Requirements (met):**

-   Receipt of unemployment benefit I with a remaining entitlement of at least 150 days
-   Viable business concept (this business plan)
-   Personal and professional suitability (demonstrated by qualifications)
-   Statement from a competent body (e.g., Cologne Business)

**Significance for the founding:**

-   Securing cost of living during the start-up phase
-   Enables focus on product development and market build-up
-   Significantly reduces financial risk
-   Additionally: continuation of social insurances

## Response to the Cologne Business Checklist {#sec-cologne-business}

The following overview addresses the central questions of the Cologne Business checklist for founding preparation:

**1. Personal and professional prerequisites:**

-   [x] Comprehensive qualification in healthcare, IT, and coaching
-   [x] Commercial knowledge through B.Sc. Health Economics and managing director experience
-   [x] Industry experience: 20+ years in healthcare, 5+ years in health IT/Data Science
-   [x] Family backing in place
-   [x] Time commitments calculated and accepted

**2. Founding concept and market opportunities:**

-   [x] Clearly defined offering: §20 apps, coaching, AI services
-   [x] Market need: growing market for digital health
-   [x] Target groups defined: private individuals, health insurance funds, WHM
-   [x] Marketing concept: freemium strategy, online marketing

**3. Competitive situation:**

-   [x] Competitors known and analyzed
-   [x] Prices calculated in line with the market
-   [x] Unique selling points clearly defined: interdisciplinary expertise, AI integration
-   [x] Sales methods: SaaS platform, direct and via health insurance funds

**4. Location choice:**

-   [x] Digital business model: location-independent
-   [x] Home office + flexible co-working space in Cologne
-   [x] Customer base reachable nationwide / internationally
-   [x] Costs minimal: €100 monthly for co-working

**5. Legal form:**

-   Sole proprietorship / freelancer (coach, IT service provider)
-   Trade registration for digital products
-   Possible later conversion into a GmbH in the case of strong growth

**6. Permits:**

-   Trade registration required
-   §20 SGB V certification for prevention courses (process known)
-   Data protection: GDPR compliance as a health service provider

**7. Profitability:**

-   [x] Detailed profitability forecast
-   [x] Break-even from Year 3 planned
-   [x] Costs fully calculated (private + business)

**8. Financing:**

-   [x] Capital requirement determined: €7,500
-   [x] Equity: €22,500
-   [x] Founding subsidy applied for: €12,000
-   [x] Liquidity secured through side income (lecturing)

**9. Insurance:**

-   Professional liability insurance (coach/IT consultant): €40 monthly
-   Private health insurance: €363 monthly
-   Pension insurance: €00.00 monthly
    -   Retirement provision through my private property in Malawi (my wife's home country, with low cost of living)

\clearpage
